title: EDSI 2025/11/06
displayTitle: 2025-11-06 - Economic Development, Science and Innovation Committee - Briefing on artificial intelligence
committee: Economic Development, Science and Innovation Committee
description: Briefing on artificial intelligence Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment Dean Ford, General Manager, Technology and Innovation Simon Rae, Manager, Digital Futures Policy
descriptionSummary: Briefing on artificial intelligence. Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment. Dean Ford, General Manager, Technology and Innovation

chunks.text: Not sure if you can answer this, but the question, first question really is how effective you think the guidance has been? Um, and you've talked about just over 2000 downloads. So it's a really hard question to Yeah, to know. So from a, uh, I guess a slight, um, uh, slight slower start, you're seeing this, this big uptake and we haven't seen any cases where there's been, um, I guess poor use of ai. So it's encouraging there, but, uh, but we have no specific information on, on, uh, AO business users yet.

Sure. The, the thing that we're doing is more helping probably some of the laggards or the more nervous kind of smaller businesses. What did the, um, data ethics and innovation tell you about the challenges that businesses would face using Maori data? Uh, so, um, uh, the, the, we, we lent on their expertise there, so we're, we're not the, the data experts. Um, but what they, there's probably two elements.

One, one was, um, uh, data is something that should be protected, whether it's multi data or any data. And, and so businesses should be careful that they're not, um, breaching any privacy concerns. But some of the sorts of things that, that they had is, um, they have a principle of, um, when using Maori data businesses should ensure they do no harm and, um, ensure that they're actually doing something that's, that's positive for Maori. So that there's both a perspective of not doing something untoward and also looking for opportunities.

And some of the principles they talk about is, um, you know, if, if you are a business that, um, I dunno, it was obviously leaning into Maori data, so you were doing something like a tedo education tool or something like that. Um, there, there was some, some, um, important steps you'd take. company directors as well.
